sections.content: The orange object was clicked. The animation shows objects at (37,46) and (19,39) changed. Let me check the current state to see what moved.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== Key colored centers ===")
for r in range(len(ascii_grid)):
    for c in range(len(ascii_grid[r])):
        ch = ascii_grid[r][c]
        if ch in 'MO':
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): {ch}")

# Check purple pixels
print("\n=== Purple pixels ===")
for r in range(len(ascii_grid)):
    for c in range(len(ascii_grid[r])):
        if ascii_grid[r][c] == 'p':
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): p")

# Check orange object area
print("\n=== Orange object area ===")
for r in range(13, 20):
    line = ascii_grid[r][35:42]
    if any(c != 'B' for c in line):
        print(f"Row {r}: {line}")

# Check purple square area
print("\n=== Purple square area ===")
for r in range(37, 46):
    line = ascii_grid[r][46:52]
    if any(c != 'B' for c in line):
        print(f"Row {r}: {line}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The purple square moved from (39-43, 47-51) to (23-27, 39-43) with M center now at (25, 41). The orange object is still at (14-18, 36-40) with M at (16, 38).
So the pattern is:
1. Click ring center → white diamond moves to ring
2. Click gray diamond center → gray diamond moves to ring
3. Click purple square center → two new p centers appear
4. Click p centers → white diamonds appear at those positions
5. Click orange object center → purple square moves to new position
Now the purple square has a new M center at (25, 41). I should click that next, following the Level 1 pattern where after an object moves, you click its new center.
